heading: Key Takeaway
text: Tissue is a group of cells similar in structure and function.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Plant tissues are of two main types – meristematic and permanent.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Meristematic tissue is the dividing tissue present in the growing regions of the plant.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Permanent tissues are derived from meristematic tissue once they lose the ability to divide. They are classified as simple and complex tissues.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma are three types of simple tissues. Xylem and phloem are types of complex tissues.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Animal tissues can be epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissue.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Depending on shape and function, epithelial tissue is classified as squamous, cuboidal, columnar, ciliated and glandular.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The different types of connective tissues in our body include areolar tissue, adipose tissue, bone, tendon, ligament, cartilage and blood.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Striated, unstriated and cardiac are three types of muscle tissues.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Nervous tissue is made of neurons that receive and conduct impulses.
